checks.description: Root element is a div with class .accordion and border-top divider
checks.notes: Matches Figma anatomy. Pending visual QA.
Required for native keyboard activation (Enter, Space) and accessible semantics.

checks.description: Panel has role=region and aria-labelledby pointing to trigger id
checks.notes: Required by ARIA Authoring Practices Guide accordion pattern.
Icon is decorative — must not be read by screen readers.

checks.description: aria-expanded reflects panel open/closed state on the trigger button
checks.notes: Value must toggle true/false on user activation.

checks.description: aria-controls on trigger references panel id
checks.notes: Links trigger to its controlled panel.

checks.description: Focus indicator is visible and meets minimum 2px contrast requirement
checks.notes: Figma specifies 2px solid #161616 (black) on trigger — high contrast focus ring.

checks.description: Disabled state uses [disabled] attribute on button
checks.notes: Native disabled prevents interaction and communicates state to assistive technology.
Pointer users do not see focus ring; keyboard users do.

checks.description: All visual values reference component-level semantic tokens
checks.notes: Component-level tokens (--accordion-*) are defined in accordion-new.css but are NOT yet present in token.css. Global token fallbacks used. Requires token.css update.

checks.description: No hardcoded hex or pixel values in component selectors
checks.notes: Hardcoded fallback values present in CSS custom property declarations. These are acceptable fallbacks until token.css is updated. No hardcodes in selectors.

checks.description: All 5 states (default, hovered, pressed, focused, disabled) are implemented
checks.notes: All states mapped to CSS selectors.

checks.description: Expanded and collapsed states are implemented via aria-expanded
checks.notes: Panel display toggled by CSS selector targeting aria-expanded on adjacent trigger.

checks.description: Non-flush focus ring implemented as inset outline on trigger
Flush focus ring bleeds to fill parent container width
checks.notes: Implemented via ::after with negative inset-inline. Requires parent overflow:clip. Approximate — pending design team verification.

checks.description: RTL layout is supported via CSS logical properties
checks.notes: All horizontal spacing uses padding-inline, margin-inline, inset-inline. No explicit RTL class needed.
